The lightbox CSS uses `position: fixed; inset: 0;` which should cover the viewport. But if any ancestor has `transform`, `filter`, `perspective`, `backdrop-filter`, or `will-change: transform`, it creates a containing block for fixed elements — anchoring the lightbox to that ancestor rather than the viewport. That's the classic cause of "fixed modal showing at the very end of the page."

content.text:
Done. Root cause and fix:

**Why it broke:** The `Lightbox` was rendered as a child of `.pdp` with `position: fixed; inset: 0`. Some ancestor in the render tree (`backdrop-filter` on `.buyBox`, plus `perspective`/`will-change` on the gallery cards as siblings, and any future transform anywhere up the tree) creates a containing block that traps `position: fixed`, anchoring the modal to that block instead of the viewport — so it appeared at the very bottom of the page.

**Fix in `ProductDetailPage.tsx:267-302`:**
- `Lightbox` is now portaled to `document.body` via `createPortal`, immune to any ancestor containing block.
- Body + html `overflow` are locked to `hidden` while open and restored on close (mobile touch and Lenis wheel can't bleed through anymore).

**Fix in `ProductDetailPage.module.css:963-1058`:**
- `.lightbox` got `height: 100dvh` + `width: 100vw` so the close button stays above the mobile URL bar.
- Image now uses `object-fit: contain`.
- New `@media (max-width: 768px)` block: 40px buttons, tighter padding, edge-tucked nav arrows, and a max-height: 80% image so prev/next/close stay tap-reachable.

Done. The blur is now applied via a different mechanism that bypasses `backdrop-filter`'s stacking-context issues entirely:

**`globals.css`** — added a global rule: when `body` has class `overlay-blur`, every direct child of body except the one with `data-overlay` gets `filter: blur(12px) saturate(70%)` and `pointer-events: none`. Because the lightbox/drawer portal as siblings of the page tree under `body`, this blurs the entire page in one stroke and leaves the overlay sharp.

**`ProductDetailPage.tsx`** — the Lightbox `useEffect` now adds/removes `body.overlay-blur` alongside the scroll lock. The lightbox div carries `data-overlay=""` so it stays sharp.

**`Reviews.tsx`** — same wiring. The portal contents are wrapped in a single `<div data-overlay="">` so React's two siblings (backdrop + drawer) both stay sharp.

**`ProductDetailPage.module.css`** — softened the lightbox's own tint to `rgba(20,20,20,0.4)` and removed the now-redundant `backdrop-filter`. The page underneath is genuinely blurred this time (real CSS `filter`, not `backdrop-filter`), so the soft tint is all that's needed on top.
